fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 41-year-old African American male with schizophrenia for follow-up.
fields.Subjective: Patient arrived appearing casual and cooperative. Reports taking olanzapine 10 mg nightly consistently using evening routine with girlfriend's reminder. States doing okay. Experiences occasional whispers once or twice weekly which he dismisses. No other hallucinations or paranoid thoughts. Sleep good at 7 hours nightly. Appetite healthy, weight stable at 183 lbs. Working part-time as delivery driver, steady job for eight months. Mood 'alright' rated 6/10. Lives with girlfriend who is supportive. Enjoys playing dominoes with friends. Attends NA meetings weekly, one year clean from cocaine. Building stable life. No safety concerns, denies SI/HI. (Seen by Clarence Washington, LCSW, Licensed Clinical Social Worker)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Casually dressed, good grooming, calm presentation

Laboratory_Data.Recent_Lab_Results: CBC and CMP within normal limits.

fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives with girlfriend; working part-time; one year clean; attending NA; building stability.
Assessment_and_Plan: Good medication adherence with girlfriend's support.
fields.Author: Note written by Clarence Washington, LCSW, Licensed Clinical Social Worker — Brothers Recovery Network, 3421 Unity Boulevard, Detroit, MI 48201, Phone: (555) 661-3487, Fax: (555) 661-3488
